# ELT Pipeline Task: airline

## Data Sources (config.yaml)
```yaml
Airbyte:
  config:
    files_definition_id: 778daa7c-feaf-4db6-96f3-70fd645acc77
    namespace_definition: destination
    postgres_definition_id: decd338e-5647-4c0b-adf4-da0e75f5a750
    s3_definition_id: 69589781-7828-43c5-9f63-8925b1c1ccc2
    server_url: http://airbyte-abctl-control-plane:80/api/public/v1/
    snowflake_definition_id: 424892c4-daac-4491-b35d-c6688ba547ba
aws_s3:
  AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: test

- format: jsonl
```

## Target Data Model (data_model.yaml)
```yaml
models:
- columns:
  - description: The airline code.
    name: airline_code
  - description: The airline name.
    name: airline_name
  - description: the flight number of the flight operated by the airline that had
      the longest delay in departure, with ties broken by the ascending order of the
      flight number
    name: flight_number_had_the_lonest_delay_in_departure
  - description: the number of flights operated by the airline in 2018, replace NULL
      values with 0.
    name: num_flights_operate_in_2018
  - description: the number of planes operated by the airline, replace NULL values
      with 0.
    name: num_planes
  - description: the number of non-cancelled flights operated by the airline, replace
      NULL values with 0.
    name: number_of_non_cancelled_flights
  - description: the total hours delayed of the airline due to a late aircraft in
      2018, replace NULL values with 0.
    name: total_hours_delayed_due_to_a_late_aircraft_in_2018
  description: Each record represents a airline.
  name: airline__airlines
- columns:
  - description: The airport code.
    name: airport_code
  - description: The airport name.
    name: airport_name
  - description: the number of flights that depart from the airport, replace NULL
      values with 0.
    name: num_flights_departure
  - description: the airline that flew the most flights to the airport, with ties
      broken by the ascending order of the airline name
    name: airline_flew_most_flights
  - description: the number of flights that landed at the airport in 2018, replace
      NULL values with 0.
    name: num_flights_landed_in_2018
  - description: the highest delay in minutes due to security among flights departing
      from the airport
    name: highest_delay_in_minutes_due_to_security
  - description: the number of cancelled flights that depart from the airport, replace
      NULL values with 0.
    name: num_cancelled_flights
  description: Each record represents a airport.
  name: airline__airports

```

# ELT Pipeline Solution for Airline Data

## 1. Terraform Configuration (main.tf)

```hcl
terraform {
  required_providers {
    airbyte = {
      source  = "airbytehq/airbyte"
      version = "~> 0.6"
    }
  }
}

provider "airbyte" {
  server_url = "http://airbyte-abctl-control-plane:80/api/public/v1/"
}

# ============================================
# SOURCES
# ============================================

# Flat Files Source - Air Carriers
resource "airbyte_source_file" "air_carriers" {
  name         = "airline_air_carriers_flat_file"
  workspace_id = var.workspace_id

  configuration = {
    url            = "https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1tRfQsfJe8ZEqDYoA0Cn4i3sZNsfh88m4"
    format         = "jsonl"
    dataset_name   = "Air_Carriers"
    provider = {
      https_public_web = {}
    }
  }
}

# Postgres Source - Airlines
resource "airbyte_source_postgres" "airlines" {
  name         = "airline_postgres_source"
  workspace_id = var.workspace_id

  configuration = {
    host     = "elt-postgres"
    port     = 5432
    database = "airline"
    username = "postgres"
    password = "testelt"
    schemas  = ["public"]
    ssl_mode = {
      disable = {}
    }
    replication_method = {
      scan_changes_with_user_defined_cursor = {}
    }
  }
}

# S3 Source - Airports
resource "airbyte_source_s3" "airports" {
  name         = "airline_s3_source"
  workspace_id = var.workspace_id

  configuration = {
    bucket                = "airline-bucket"
    aws_access_key_id     = "test"
    aws_secret_access_key = "test"
    endpoint              = "http://elt-localstack:4566"
    region_name           = "us-west-2"
    streams = [
      {
        name         = "Airports"
        format = {
          jsonl_format = {}
        }
        globs = ["Airports.jsonl"]
      }
    ]
  }
}

# ============================================
# DESTINATION
# ============================================

resource "airbyte_destination_snowflake" "snowflake" {
  name         = "airline_snowflake_destination"
  workspace_id = var.workspace_id

  configuration = {
    host               = "your-account.snowflakecomputing.com"
    database           = "airline"
    schema             = "AIRBYTE_SCHEMA"
    warehouse          = "AIRBYTE_WAREHOUSE"
    role               = "AIRBYTE_ROLE"
    username           = "AIRBYTE_USER"
    credentials = {
      password = {
        password = "Snowflake@123"
      }
    }
  }
}

# ============================================
# CONNECTIONS
# ============================================

# Connection: Flat Files (Air Carriers) -> Snowflake
resource "airbyte_connection" "air_carriers_to_snowflake" {
  name           = "airline_air_carriers_connection"
  source_id      = airbyte_source_file.air_carriers.source_id
  destination_id = airbyte_destination_snowflake.snowflake.destination_id

  namespace_definition = "destination"
  namespace_format     = "AIRBYTE_SCHEMA"

  configurations = {
    streams = [
      {
        name      = "Air_Carriers"
        sync_mode = "full_refresh_append"
      }
    ]
  }

  schedule = {
    schedule_type = "manual"
  }
}

# Connection: Postgres (Airlines) -> Snowflake
resource "airbyte_connection" "postgres_to_snowflake" {
  name           = "airline_postgres_connection"
  source_id      = airbyte_source_postgres.airlines.source_id
  destination_id = airbyte_destination_snowflake.snowflake.destination_id

  namespace_definition = "destination"
  namespace_format     = "AIRBYTE_SCHEMA"

  configurations = {
    streams = [
      {
        name      = "airlines"
        sync_mode = "full_refresh_append"
      }
    ]
  }

  schedule = {
    schedule_type = "manual"
  }
}

# Connection: S3 (Airports) -> Snowflake
resource "airbyte_connection" "s3_to_snowflake" {
  name           = "airline_s3_connection"
  source_id      = airbyte_source_s3.airports.source_id
  destination_id = airbyte_destination_snowflake.snowflake.destination_id

  namespace_definition = "destination"
  namespace_format     = "AIRBYTE_SCHEMA"

  configurations = {
    streams = [
      {
        name      = "Airports"
        sync_mode = "full_refresh_append"
      }
    ]
  }

  schedule = {
    schedule_type = "manual"
  }
}

# ============================================
# VARIABLES
# ============================================

variable "workspace_id" {
  description = "Airbyte workspace ID"
  type        = string
}

# ============================================
# OUTPUTS
# ============================================

output "air_carriers_connection_id" {
  value = airbyte_connection.air_carriers_to_snowflake.connection_id
}

output "postgres_connection_id" {
  value = airbyte_connection.postgres_to_snowflake.connection_id
}

output "s3_connection_id" {
  value = airbyte_connection.s3_to_snowflake.connection_id
}
```

## 2. SQL Transformations

### airline__airlines.sql

```sql
-- Model: airline__airlines
-- Description: Each record represents an airline with aggregated metrics

WITH air_carriers AS (
    SELECT 
        Code AS airline_code,
        Description AS airline_name
    FROM {{ source('airbyte', 'Air_Carriers') }}
),

airlines_data AS (
    SELECT * 
    FROM {{ source('airbyte', 'airlines') }}
),

-- Get flight with longest departure delay per airline
longest_delay_flights AS (
    SELECT 
        OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID,
        OP_CARRIER_FL_NUM,
        DEP_DELAY,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID 
            ORDER BY DEP_DELAY DESC, OP_CARRIER_FL_NUM ASC
        ) AS rn
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE DEP_DELAY IS NOT NULL
),

flight_with_longest_delay AS (
    SELECT 
        OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID,
        OP_CARRIER_FL_NUM AS flight_number_had_the_lonest_delay_in_departure
    FROM longest_delay_flights
    WHERE rn = 1
),

-- Count flights in 2018
flights_in_2018 AS (
    SELECT 
        OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID,
        COUNT(*) AS num_flights_operate_in_2018
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE(FL_DATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD')) = 2018
    GROUP BY OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID
),

-- Count distinct planes per airline
planes_per_airline AS (
    SELECT 
        OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID,
        COUNT(DISTINCT TAIL_NUM) AS num_planes
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE TAIL_NUM IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID
),

-- Count non-cancelled flights
non_cancelled_flights AS (
    SELECT 
        OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID,
        COUNT(*) AS number_of_non_cancelled_flights
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE CANCELLED = 0 OR CANCELLED IS NULL
    GROUP BY OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID
),

-- Total hours delayed due to late aircraft in 2018
late_aircraft_delay_2018 AS (
    SELECT 
        OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID,
        SUM(COALESCE(LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY, 0)) / 60.0 AS total_hours_delayed_due_to_a_late_aircraft_in_2018
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE(FL_DATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD')) = 2018
    GROUP BY OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID
)

SELECT 
    ac.airline_code,
    ac.airline_name,
    fld.flight_number_had_the_lonest_delay_in_departure,
    COALESCE(f18.num_flights_operate_in_2018, 0) AS num_flights_operate_in_2018,
    COALESCE(ppa.num_planes, 0) AS num_planes,
    COALESCE(ncf.number_of_non_cancelled_flights, 0) AS number_of_non_cancelled_flights,
    COALESCE(lad.total_hours_delayed_due_to_a_late_aircraft_in_2018, 0) AS total_hours_delayed_due_to_a_late_aircraft_in_2018
FROM air_carriers ac
LEFT JOIN flight_with_longest_delay fld 
    ON CAST(ac.airline_code AS VARCHAR) = CAST(fld.OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID AS VARCHAR)
LEFT JOIN flights_in_2018 f18 
    ON CAST(ac.airline_code AS VARCHAR) = CAST(f18.OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID AS VARCHAR)
LEFT JOIN planes_per_airline ppa 
    ON CAST(ac.airline_code AS VARCHAR) = CAST(ppa.OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID AS VARCHAR)
LEFT JOIN non_cancelled_flights ncf 
    ON CAST(ac.airline_code AS VARCHAR) = CAST(ncf.OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID AS VARCHAR)
LEFT JOIN late_aircraft_delay_2018 lad 
    ON CAST(ac.airline_code AS VARCHAR) = CAST(lad.OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID AS VARCHAR)
```

### airline__airports.sql

```sql
-- Model: airline__airports
-- Description: Each record represents an airport with aggregated metrics

WITH airports AS (
    SELECT 
        Code AS airport_code,
        Description AS airport_name
    FROM {{ source('airbyte', 'Airports') }}
),

airlines_data AS (
    SELECT * 
    FROM {{ source('airbyte', 'airlines') }}
),

air_carriers AS (
    SELECT 
        Code AS airline_code,
        Description AS airline_name
    FROM {{ source('airbyte', 'Air_Carriers') }}
),

-- Count flights departing from each airport
departure_flights AS (
    SELECT 
        ORIGIN AS airport_code,
        COUNT(*) AS num_flights_departure
    FROM airlines_data
    GROUP BY ORIGIN
),

-- Find airline that flew most flights TO each airport (destination)
flights_to_airport AS (
    SELECT 
        DEST AS airport_code,
        OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID,
        COUNT(*) AS flight_count
    FROM airlines_data
    GROUP BY DEST, OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID
),

ranked_airlines AS (
    SELECT 
        fta.airport_code,
        ac.airline_name,
        fta.flight_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY fta.airport_code 
            ORDER BY fta.flight_count DESC, ac.airline_name ASC
        ) AS rn
    FROM flights_to_airport fta
    LEFT JOIN air_carriers ac 
        ON CAST(fta.OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID AS VARCHAR) = CAST(ac.airline_code AS VARCHAR)
),

airline_most_flights AS (
    SELECT 
        airport_code,
        airline_name AS airline_flew_most_flights
    FROM ranked_airlines
    WHERE rn = 1
),

-- Count flights landed in 2018
flights_landed_2018 AS (
    SELECT 
        DEST AS airport_code,
        COUNT(*) AS num_flights_landed_in_2018
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE(FL_DATE, 'YYYY-MM-DD')) = 2018
    GROUP BY DEST
),

-- Highest security delay for departing flights
security_delay AS (
    SELECT 
        ORIGIN AS airport_code,
        MAX(SECURITY_DELAY) AS highest_delay_in_minutes_due_to_security
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE SECURITY_DELAY IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY ORIGIN
),

-- Count cancelled flights departing from airport
cancelled_flights AS (
    SELECT 
        ORIGIN AS airport_code,
        COUNT(*) AS num_cancelled_flights
    FROM airlines_data
    WHERE CANCELLED = 1
    GROUP BY ORIGIN
)

SELECT 
    ap.airport_code,
    ap.airport_name,
    COALESCE(df.num_flights_departure, 0) AS num_flights_departure,
    amf.airline_flew_most_flights,
    COALESCE(fl18.num_flights_landed_in_2018, 0) AS num_flights_landed_in_2018,
    sd.highest_delay_in_minutes_due_to_security,
    COALESCE(cf.num_cancelled_flights, 0) AS num_cancelled_flights
FROM airports ap
LEFT JOIN departure_flights df 
    ON ap.airport_code = df.airport_code
LEFT JOIN airline_most_flights amf 
    ON ap.airport_code = amf.airport_code
LEFT JOIN flights_landed_2018 fl18 
    ON ap.airport_code = fl18.airport_code
LEFT JOIN security_delay sd 
    ON ap.airport_code = sd.airport_code
LEFT JOIN cancelled_flights cf 
    ON ap.airport_code = cf.airport_code
```

## Summary

This solution provides:

1. **Terraform Configuration**: Sets up three Airbyte connections:
   - Flat file source (Google Drive) for Air Carriers data
   - PostgreSQL source for Airlines flight data
   - S3 source (LocalStack) for Airports data
   - All data flows to Snowflake destination

2. **SQL Transformations**:
   - `airline__airlines`: Aggregates airline metrics including flight counts, plane counts, delays, and identifies flights with longest delays
   - `airline__airports`: Aggregates airport metrics including departure/arrival counts, identifies top airlines per airport, and tracks security delays and cancellations

expected.airline__airports: select * from airline.airbyte_schema.airline__airports order by airport_code
expected.airline__airlines: select * from airline.airbyte_schema.airline__airlines order by airline_code

evaluation_details.explanation: The SQL transformation files have the correct structure for generating SELECT queries, but the specific SQL queries do not exactly match the expected output queries. The queries in the actual output are more complex, performing multiple transformations and joins, while the expected output shows simple SELECT statements with ordering.
